Rep. Ilhan Omar (D-MN-05) fired off dozens tweets this week response President Donald Trump's Tuesday address the nation, nearly outpacing the president himself tweets about the southern border wall.
"Two children died ICE custody last month after 10+ years with such deaths. There's blood your hands," Omar tweeted Tuesday.
Tonight, while #Trump gets free airtime for propaganda the "humanitarian national security crisis" the border, CHILDREN sit cold cages inhumane conditions. There's blood your hands.
"We are literally watching manufactured crisis, designed divert attention from this criminal and dysfunctional administration. Stay woke America, Individual-1 not one sleep on," she later wrote. Notably, Individual was code-name used federal prosecutors the charges brought against Trump's former attorney, Michael Cohen, and was later made clear that Trump and Individual are one and the same.
are literally watching manufactured crisis, designed divert attention from this criminal and dysfunctional administration.
After Trump's remarks, Omar sought fact-check the president, specifically taking issue with his numbers unaccompanied minors who crossed the border and were suspected having MS-13 affiliations.
"Border apprehensions are year low, down percent from 1.6 million 2000 396,579 2018. The facts don't lie, but Trump does. Not one dollar for his vanity wall," Omar added.

"The number one role president has protect Americans and our homeland, and today's visit the southern border President Trump further illustrates his strong commitment the safety this great nation and its people," Minnesota GOP Chairwoman Jennifer Carnahan said Thursday press release.
Background Photo "Trump Shutdown" Ilhan Omar.

The design part the complex set upgrading and territorial innovation actions promoted the Universal Exposition 2015, aimed enhancing the Lombard territory whole and, particular, ensuring the necessary access roads.
The design pursues full integration the exhibition center Expo 2015 with the territory, ensuring proper access the site means expressway with approx. length, located northwest Milan, close the new Exhibition Center Rho-Pero.
From merely road perspective, the link natural continuation the alternative route the S.S. 'Padana Superiore' the municipality Milan, originates from the intersection the latter with the S.S. 'del Sempione'.
During the upgrading stage, the route initially develops artificial tunnel passing under the area the Integrated Intervention Plan Cascina Merlata; then joins the design roads for the new junction the Milan-Turin well with primary municipal road under development the municipality Milan (interdistrict road). Subsequently, passing over the railway station Milano Certosa the Milan-Novara line, the motorway and the EXPO 2015 area, ends with partial junction the 'Milano Laghi' motorway north direction.
The layout has been designed such way that, the future, extension would possible reach the S.S. 233 'Varesina' and the 'Rho-Monza'.
The main works included the design are the artificial tunnel passing under the area the Integrated Intervention Plan Cascina Merlata and the viaduct originating south the intersected railway line and ending the motorway.
Built with the characteristic excavation method 'Milan type' (which allows minimize construction times and impact the territory during the works) and the innovative system the 'white tank' (thanks which waterproof concrete can obtained already during processing), the artificial tunnel develops for approx. km, bypassing the S.S.33 del Sempione and joining the roads leading the Western by-pass road Molino Dorino.
The system bridges and viaducts made series steel arches: for the bridges the and motorways, these elements have structural function; while along the EXPO viaduct they only have aesthetic function, aimed making the whole project look organic and well fitted into the urban context 'EXPO 2015' and creating the so-called 'Door Milan' distinctive element for those who drive the road from and the city center).
The whole elevated system made even more homogeneous and characteristic the covering 'guard' the decks, external metal coating Alucobond that ensures continuity among the works. Thanks specific lighting studies, the presence this element helps create appealing play light during the evening and the night.

reasonably tight race eastern Oregon, Grant county decided continue with ban marijuana related businesses Tuesday. The ban, which was enacted the County Court after the legislature paved the way last year, was challenged when cannabis advocates raised enough signatures place Measure 12-58 the local ballot during the primary election May 17th.
The margin for the loss was 46.4% voting yes 53.6% voting (to keep the ban). all, just over 3,000 votes were cast (est. 95% reporting). Grant county one the least populated Oregon.
Interestingly, local law enforcement and Federal agents recently raided illegal mountain cannabis grow near Wheeler. Likely run Mexican cartel with workers camping near the grow site, one was arrested and over 8,000 plants were eradicated.
similar effort, Klamath Falls Measure 18-105 also failed. Story here.

Northeast Vietnam has cool cold season (courtesy the northeast monsoon from November March) and warm hot wet season (from April October). December and January particular can get quite frosty the far north Vietnam pack accordingly. This time the year can also see heavy mists that can run for days these can reduce visibility places like Sapa and Long Bay frustratingly short distances. The wettest months the year Hanoi are July and August, the driest, December and January.

Jerry Springer says still deciding whether will run for Ohio governor 2018.
Speculation around Springer's potential gubernatorial bid has been growing for several months.
The Enquirer also reported Wednesday that Springer soliciting feedback from Ohio State Sen. Sandra Williams, Democratic Reps. Janine Boyd and Stephanie Howse, and political consultants.
"Glad attendance rep's Stephanie Howse and Janine Boyd fundraiser w/Jerry Springer, possible candidate for Gov. Ohio," Williams tweeted Tuesday.
"He's very serious," Williams said Springer's interest running, interview with Cleveland.com.
The fundraiser was held Nighttown, Cleveland Heights jazz club and restaurant.
Ring said Springer gave brief speech the fundraiser.
Springer, 73, previously considered running for Senate 2000 and 2004, but decided against it.
has previously attributed the rumors around his potential bid for governor former reality star Donald Trump's successful White House bid.
But people continued discuss Springer's potential return politics.
late May, Business Insider, citing more than half dozen Democrats familiar with the race, reported influential Ohio Democrats including former Ohio Gov. Ted Strickland and Hamilton County Democratic Party Chairman Tim Burke pushed for Springer run.
interview with CNN's Brooke Baldwin May 2016, Springer who supported Democrat Hillary Clinton 2016 was seemingly unsurprised Trump's popularity. said that since President Ronald Reagan, generation Americans has grown believing government the problem America.
"The celebrity politics was inevitable," Springer said.
Since Trump's win, growing number celebrities have expressed interest running for office, have not ruled out pursuing politics the future.
think it's real possibility," Johnson told the publication when asked would ever run.
July, West Virginia resident created campaign committee called "Run The Rock 2020" draft Johnson presidential candidate 2020.
Musician Kid Rock teased potential bid for Senate, but ultimately used the social media hype around the buzz announce his non-profit designed promote voter registration.

The Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) delaying the effective date the final regulations that require nutrition labelling the major cuts raw meat and poultry products and ground chopped meat and poultry products.
The original effective date for these regulations was January 2012, but the new effective date the final rule March 2012.
FSIS taking this action response request from eight trade associations, including AMI. The associations requested that FSIS exercise enforcement discretion for six month period following the January 2012, effective date the final rule. FSIS, however, concluded that two month delay the effective date will allow industry sufficient time comply with the requirements the final rule. FSIS also making correction the final rule clarify amendatory instruction.

MANCHESTER 24th April 2018: Gama, global provider business information and services specializing FMCG product innovation and international trade, has today officially opened entries for the upcoming edition the Gama Innovation Awards.
The Gama Innovation Awards recognise outstanding innovation the FMCG space across eight categories: Product, Packaging, Positioning, Branding Design, Health Wellness, SME, Best Britain and Best Middle East. Winners will announced awards ceremony that will take place Manchester's iconic Bridgewater Hall Thursday 25th October 2018.
Gama can today also reveal its panel international judges who will tasked with choosing the winning entries from shortlist innovative products each category. Representing some the leading FMCG firms across the globe, the exciting line-up judges includes thought leaders from companies such Kraft Heinz, Unilever, Carlsberg, Sonae and PepsiCo, underlining the Gama Innovation Awards' growing reputation major showcase global innovation.
Cesar Pereira, founder CEO Gama, commented: "We are thrilled return this year with expanded edition the Gama Innovation Awards. expect the event inspiring opportunity celebrate the very best global FMCG innovation. For 2018 are also delighted introduce our Best Middle East award, recognising the increased dynamism the FMCG sector the region".
Gama Innovation Award will represent fantastic accolade for every winner, the judging panel made their industry peers, including top executives from the world's leading FMCG businesses".
Entries the Gama Innovation Awards are open until Friday 15th June, and manufacturers are invited submit their own product innovations for consideration accordance with the entry requirements and qualifying criteria.
Gama global provider business information and services specializing product innovation and international trade. Through our worldwide network over 100 experts more than countries, Gama offers high-quality news, research, analysis and partnering services.
